task: I need to get ready for a school project this week, and I’m trying to keep track of what to do first, what to bring, and what can wait until later. Can you help me organize it in a way that would be easy to use while I’m working?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a simple way to organize it:

### Project Plan
**Do first**
- Read the directions
- Gather the main materials
- Start the hardest part

**Bring / keep ready**
- Notebook or paper
- Pencil
- Project materials
- Any handouts

**Can wait until later**
- Decorating
- Final checking
- Putting it in neat order

### Quick checklist
- [ ] Read directions  
- [ ] Gather materials  
- [ ] Start main work  
- [ ] Keep supplies ready  
- [ ] Save finishing touches for later  

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-page checklist** with blanks to fill in.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, but make it more specific. Like split it into today, tomorrow, and the day it’s due. And maybe add a spot for what I need to bring to school, because that part matters more than just “materials.” I want it easy to glance at, not a big paragraph.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a simple **today / tomorrow / due day** version:

### Project Plan

**Today**
- Read the directions
- Start the hardest part
- Make a list of what to bring

**Tomorrow**
- Keep working on the main part
- Check what you still need
- Pack school items

**Day it’s due**
- Finish last details
- Put everything in your bag
- Bring to school

### What to bring
- [ ] Project
- [ ] Notebook/paper
- [ ] Pencil
- [ ] Handout/directions
- [ ] Anything special for the project

### Quick check
- [ ] Started the work
- [ ] Packed what I need
- [ ] Ready for school

If you want, I can make it even shorter into a **tiny checklist format**.

user_simulator.text: Pretty close. I want it even more usable, like with a place to write the exact thing to bring, not just “project” or “anything special.” And maybe put the due day part first, because that’s the part I’d check fastest. Like a little checklist I can glance at before school.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a more usable, glance-fast version:

### Project Checklist

**Due day**
- [ ] Finish last details
- [ ] Put project in bag
- [ ] Bring: __________
- [ ] Bring: __________

**Today**
- [ ] Read directions
- [ ] Start hardest part
- [ ] Write what I still need: __________

**Tomorrow**
- [ ] Keep working
- [ ] Check what’s missing
- [ ] Pack school items

### Quick check
- [ ] I know what to bring
- [ ] I started the work
- [ ] I’m ready for school

If you want, I can make it into an even **smaller box-style checklist**.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. The due day first makes it easier to check fast, and the blank lines for exactly what to bring are better than the vague stuff. Good enough.
